body.u: looking back I don’t think that i have any bad memories of my past, from the age of 17 i have had three steady jobs, I know that I was very unsettled at first I couldn’t get a job that I really liked and could be suited to, I think the most happy thing to me was when I pastpassed my driving test I remeberremember the joy of driving back home in the car bursting to tell everyone.

Although at the time I didn’t enjoy my work in the large faversham firm I am pleased to say that achieved a good working position before leaving to come here to BromelyBromley. I don’t regret getting married at a fairly early age 22, I was glad to find a person to whom I confide in and who would confide in me.

My work that i have done over the years has pleased me, I am glad to say that I never did anything drastically wrong, and was able to keep calm, I can remeberremember sitting in the class room at school, being very frightened or at least dubious of what mistakes i would make in the future years of working life.

In the early years i was a little saddened at not getting the right qualifications in the fifth year at Comprehensive school, I did want so much at that particular time to go to Art College for a couple of years training to become a Commercial Artist or a Window dresser. I have, however, released my artistic talents on the interior and exterior of the houses I have lived in over the last 50 years, people who have visited my homes in this time period have complementedcomplimented me, so I am pleased to say that I have gained in this way what I thought was so lost 52 years ago.

I am pleased to say that I have coped fairly well with saving of mortgages and savings when my family were young, saving for holidays and luxuries. I was able to appreciate the value of money at the age of 19 this is now a pleasing thought as I look back.

I think the most amusing exciting and amusing time in my life was when I got to work at Gatwick airport in my 29th to 32nd years, the team in the office were friendly and helpful and I am glad to say that I havent lost touch with any of them; they are all goof friends of mine.

I can remember sitting at school and always wondering what my life would turn out to be like, wondering if I were to marry soon or a little later or not at all, if I were to have children or not, so many different things used to occur to me.
I am glad that my life has turned out well and I have had a nice home and luxuries that some unfortunate people that I knew early on in life have missed.
